name: Azud de Pareja from Sacedón — circular by the Embalse de Entrepeñas
name_original: Azud de Pareja desde Sacedón — circular por el Embalse de Entrepeñas
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Sacedón navigate a landscape shaped by the Embalse de Entrepeñas and Buendía Reservoir, offering diverse terrain. The region features rolling hills and varied elevations, with routes often following the contours of the large reservoirs. This area provides a mix of open stretches and more challenging ascents, suitable for different cycling preferences. Best touring cycling routes around Sacedón The most popular touring cycling route is Azud de Pareja from Sacedón — circular by the Embalse de Entrepeñas, a 30.7 miles (49.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 15 minutes to complete. This route offers extensive views of the Entrepeñas Reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Alhóndiga y Auñón desde Sacedón — circular por el Embalse del Entrepeñas, a difficult 39.8 miles (64.1 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and panoramic reservoir scenery. Local touring cyclists also love the Route of the Faces and Buendía Reservoir — circular from Buendía, a 14.6 miles (23.5 km) trail leading through the unique "Route of the Faces" sculptures and along the Buendía Reservoir, often completed in about 1 hour 52 minutes. Touring cycling around Sacedón is defined by large reservoirs, rolling hills, and varied elevation gains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to advanced cycling routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 57 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Sacedón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Sacedón?
A: There are over 15 touring cycling routes around Sacedón, offering a variety of experiences. These routes navigate a landscape shaped by the Embalse de Entrepeñas and Buendía Reservoir, with options for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Sacedón?
A: While many routes in Sacedón feature significant elevation changes, there is at least one easy touring cycling route. For those looking for a moderate option, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086262219/route-of-the-faces-and-buendia-reservoir-circular-from-buendia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces and Buendía Reservoir — circular from Buendía&lt;/a&gt; is a good choice, covering 14.6 miles (23.5 km) with manageable elevation.

Q: What are the most challenging touring cycling routes in the Sacedón area?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1189332803/alhondiga-y-aunon-desde-sacedon-circular-por-el-embalse-del-entrepenas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alhóndiga y Auñón desde Sacedón — circular por el Embalse del Entrepeñas&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gain over 39.8 miles (64.1 km). Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1189362774/azud-de-pareja-from-sacedon-circular-by-the-embalse-de-entrepenas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Azud de Pareja from Sacedón — circular by the Embalse de Entrepeñas&lt;/a&gt;, which is 30.7 miles (49.4 km) long with substantial climbs.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Sacedón?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in Sacedón are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Popular circular options include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1189362774/azud-de-pareja-from-sacedon-circular-by-the-embalse-de-entrepenas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Azud de Pareja from Sacedón — circular by the Embalse de Entrepeñas&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1095106500/castejon-circular-from-canaveruelas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castejón — circular from Cañaveruelas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of elevation gain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Sacedón?
A: The Sacedón region features rolling hills and varied elevations. While some routes are moderate, many involve significant climbing. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1189332803/alhondiga-y-aunon-desde-sacedon-circular-por-el-embalse-del-entrepenas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alhóndiga y Auñón desde Sacedón — circular por el Embalse del Entrepeñas&lt;/a&gt; route includes nearly 800 meters of ascent.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions to see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes often pass by significant points of interest. You can explore the unique sculptures of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3905216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy panoramic views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5398646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña de la Virgen Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4440584" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrepeñas Dam Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3906128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buendía Dam&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4072197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Azud de Pareja&lt;/a&gt; are also notable features of the reservoir landscape.

Q: Is it possible to find parking near the touring cycling trailheads in Sacedón?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in the towns and villages around Sacedón, such as Sacedón itself or Buendía, which serve as common starting points for many routes. Look for designated parking areas or street parking in these locations.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that are particularly scenic or offer great views of the reservoirs?
A: Many routes in the Sacedón area are designed to showcase the stunning reservoir scenery. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1189362774/azud-de-pareja-from-sacedon-circular-by-the-embalse-de-entrepenas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Azud de Pareja from Sacedón — circular by the Embalse de Entrepeñas&lt;/a&gt; offers extensive views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1189332803/alhondiga-y-aunon-desde-sacedon-circular-por-el-embalse-del-entrepenas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alhóndiga y Auñón desde Sacedón — circular por el Embalse del Entrepeñas&lt;/a&gt; route is known for its panoramic reservoir vistas.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Sacedón?
A: Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for touring cycling in Sacedón, with milder temperatures and less extreme weather. Summers can be very hot, especially in July and August, while winters can be chilly, though cycling is still possible on clear days.

Q: Can I find any routes that pass by historical sites?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate historical elements. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142426" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medieval Bridge of Auñón loop from Sacedón&lt;/a&gt; takes you past a significant historical structure, offering a blend of natural beauty and cultural heritage.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Sacedón?
A: The touring cycling routes around Sacedón are well-regarded by the komoot community. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the extensive views of the Entrepeñas and Buendía reservoirs, and the variety of routes available for different ability levels.
tips: Sacedón is a small town on the banks of the Entrepeñas Reservoir. A great place to start your adventure. Head over to the Plaza de la Constitución to eat something and rest in the shadow of the town church.

The path crosses the Tagus River over the Pareja bridge and takes the Alocén detour along a panoramic road between mountains of oaks, Pyrenean oaks and pine forests. The first kilometers to the town are a comfortable and quiet climb of about seven kilometers and with hardly any traffic. The route continues along the same road and immediately begins a fantastic descent through the pine forests of Monte de la Veguilla to the tunnels of the Entrepeñas dam. Among the trees appear the calcareous cliffs of the island of Pico de Monte Abajo and at the end the steep islet of Boca del Infierno, a symbolic stamp in the landscapes of the Alcarreño swamp. On the south side of the dam, after passing through the tunnel, there are the viewpoints of the Romanesque Bridge and Castillejo, impressive, you have to stop, lean out and contemplate the great calcareous canyon of the Tagus River in this environment, thinking about the aspect that should have before the construction of the Entrepeñas reservoir in 1956.
The medieval bridge of Auñón crosses the Tagus river near the Entrepeñas reservoir. It was built in the 14th century and is more than 80 meters long.
Great walk through the incredibly diverse landscape created around the Entrepeñas Reservoir located near the city of Sacedón.
Magnificent arched viaduct built over the Tagus River and the Entrepeñas Reservoir.

A nature reserve of incredible beauty created by the waters of nearby Pareja. You can cycle around the lake to enjoy the natural oasis in the Alcarria Alta. I recommend stopping for a swim on a hot summer day.
